intro: Ivesia eremica, also known as the Great Basin ivesia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Great Basin region of North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate symptoms associated with various diseases. Bioactive constituents of Ivesia eremica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties.
